subject: GeneralKnowledge
title: GeneralKnowledge Test - 24
description: A mix of fun and educational questions covering animals, geography, science, health, and technology for Grade 4 students.

questions.q: Which of these animals is known for having a pouch to carry its baby?
questions.explanation: Kangaroos are marsupials, and mothers carry their young, known as joeys, in a pouch on their belly.

questions.q: What is the capital city of Australia?
questions.explanation: Canberra was chosen as the capital of Australia as a compromise between the two largest cities, Sydney and Melbourne.

questions.q: Which part of a plant helps it breathe by taking in gases?
questions.explanation: Leaves have tiny pores that allow the plant to take in carbon dioxide and release oxygen.
questions.hint: They are usually flat and green.

questions.q: What do we call a person who designs houses and buildings?
questions.explanation: An architect creates the plans and designs for buildings to ensure they are safe and functional.

questions.q: Which of these is a healthy habit to keep your heart strong?
questions.explanation: Physical exercise like running helps to strengthen the heart and lungs.
questions.hint: It is a fun physical activity.

questions.q: Which community helper works at a fire station?
questions.explanation: Firefighters work at fire stations, keeping their gear ready to respond to emergencies.
questions.hint: They use water to stop fires.

questions.q: What is the name of the planet closest to the Sun?
questions.explanation: Mercury is the smallest planet in the solar system and orbits closest to the Sun.
questions.hint: It has a name that starts with 'M'.

questions.q: Which of these is a material that does NOT conduct electricity well?
questions.explanation: Wood is an insulator, meaning electricity does not flow through it easily.

questions.q: How many minutes are in an hour?
questions.explanation: There are exactly 60 minutes in one hour.

questions.q: Which season is known for being the hottest?
questions.explanation: Summer is the warmest season, usually characterized by long, sunny days.
